executive_summary: Fixes the root cause deferred by SD-FDBK-ENH-CONFIRMED-LIVE-TODAY-001 (interim mitigation only): scripts/session-tick.cjs's steady-state PATCH filters status=eq.active (line 202); when stale-session-sweep.cjs demotes a live-quiescent session active->idle, the next tick's PATCH affects 0 rows and cleanupAndExit(0) fires, killing a healthy daemon and freezing process_alive_at. LEAD-phase sub-agents (Explore + VALIDATION) confirmed the naive fix (reverting the filter entirely) would reintroduce a DIFFERENT, previously-fixed incident: tests/unit/session-tick-release-on-exit.test.mjs FR-2 encodes the correct behavior for incident 18b90582 (an orphan tick patching a released row for 13h) -- the filter must stop on TERMINAL statuses (released/completed), not merely widen unconditionally. The surgical fix is status=in.(active,idle), preserving stop-on-released while fixing stop-on-idle. Two additional bugs share the file: rediscoverParentPid() (line 114) is structurally inert on Windows (greps Win32_Process.CommandLine for an SSE port that is only ever an env var, never a CLI arg) -- fixed by reusing capture-session-id.cjs's existing claude_sessions.pid refresh (written on every hook fire) instead of local WMI process scanning. And the first-tick merge-duplicates POST (line 165) writes status:'active' unconditionally, risking resurrection of a legitimately-released row under a race. This PRD also builds the new spawn-and-observe test infrastructure needed to prove these fixes under real Windows process semantics (existing tests are static regex-on-source-text only), and stages re-enabling LEO_DORMANCY_WATCHDOG_ENABLED=on (low blast radius, advisory-only) while explicitly deferring LEO_MASKED_STALL_DETECT=on (drives actual capacity decisions, higher blast radius) to a separate tracked follow-up with its own monitoring window.

functional_requirements.title: Widen steady-state PATCH live-status set to survive idle
functional_requirements.description: scripts/session-tick.cjs line 202: change the PATCH filter from `status=eq.active` to `status=in.(active,idle)`. The 0-row Content-Range detection (lines 217-225) and cleanupAndExit(0) call are UNCHANGED -- a row that has moved to a TERMINAL status (released/completed) still correctly returns 0 rows and stops the tick, preserving the fix for incident 18b90582. Only a live-quiescent (idle) session now survives the PATCH instead of being incorrectly treated as released.
acceptance_criteria: A live spawn-and-observe test proves the tick survives a status transition from active to idle (does not call cleanupAndExit, keeps ticking).
A live spawn-and-observe test proves the tick STILL stops on a transition to released or completed (0-row PATCH, cleanupAndExit fires) -- the original 18b90582 fix is not regressed.

tests/unit/session-tick-release-on-exit.test.mjs FR-2's existing regex assertion (line 91-101, matching the literal `status=eq.active` filter string) is UPDATED to match the new `status=in.(active,idle)` filter -- not deleted, not left failing.

functional_requirements.title: Replace structurally-inert Windows rediscoverParentPid() with a DB-based re-query
functional_requirements.description: scripts/session-tick.cjs rediscoverParentPid() (line 114-136) currently greps Win32_Process.CommandLine for CLAUDE_CODE_SSE_PORT -- structurally incapable of matching since that value is only ever process.env, never present in any process's command line anywhere in the codebase (confirmed by repo-wide grep). Replace the local WMI/PowerShell process scan with a lightweight PostgREST GET of `claude_sessions?session_id=eq.<own session_id>&select=pid`, since scripts/hooks/capture-session-id.cjs's upsertSessionRow() already refreshes this column on every hook fire (SessionStart, /clear, reconnect) -- an already-working, already-authoritative source for the current live parent PID. Adopt the queried PID as the new parentPid if it is alive (process.kill(pid,0)) and differs from the currently-pinned one.
acceptance_criteria: rediscoverParentPid() no longer shells out to PowerShell/WMI; it performs a read-only GET against claude_sessions.

A live spawn-and-observe test simulates a PID rotation (claude_sessions.pid updated to a new live PID for the same session_id) and proves the tick adopts the new PID and continues ticking instead of exiting after MAX_PARENT_MISSES.
The fix works identically cross-platform (the DB re-query has no Windows-specific dependency) -- the prior `if (process.platform !== 'win32') return 0;` guard is removed or updated accordingly.
Repo-wide grep confirms no other production code path (besides tests) depends on the removed WMI/CommandLine matching logic before it is deleted.

functional_requirements.title: Guard the first-tick merge-duplicates POST against resurrecting a released row
functional_requirements.description: scripts/session-tick.cjs's first-tick POST (line 161-193) uses `Prefer: resolution=merge-duplicates` with body `status: 'active'` unconditionally. Since this is a PostgREST UPSERT on UNIQUE(session_id), if a row was legitimately released between the daemon's spawn and its first tick (by releaseRowOnExitBestEffort or stale-session-sweep), this POST can resurrect it. Guard: perform the merge-duplicates POST WITHOUT unconditionally setting status, or add a follow-up conditional check that the row's status did not silently get overwritten from a terminal state -- the design must not introduce a second write path that itself risks a race; prefer the simplest sound fix (e.g. omit `status` from the first-tick body when the row may already exist, letting PostgREST's merge-duplicates preserve the existing value, and separately setting status:'active' only via a subsequent conditional PATCH filtered by NOT status=in.(released,completed)).
acceptance_criteria: A live spawn-and-observe test creates a released row, then simulates a first-tick fire for the same session_id, and proves the row's status remains released (not resurrected to active).
A live spawn-and-observe test proves the normal case (no prior row) still correctly self-heals within one tick, unchanged from today's behavior.

functional_requirements.title: New spawn-and-observe Windows test infrastructure
functional_requirements.description: Existing tests/unit/session-tick-*.test.mjs files are 100% static (readFileSync + regex match against source text) -- zero coverage of actual runtime behavior. Build a harness that: makes TICK_MS and PARENT_POLL_MS env-overridable so tests run in ~1-2s not 30-60s; makes the PostgREST base URL injectable so tests target a DISPOSABLE table or a strictly-scoped disposable session_id (test-tick-<uuid> prefix) with an assert-before-mutate guard that refuses to touch any row whose session_id lacks the test prefix; hard time-boxes each test at <=10s with a deterministic completion signal (not a fixed sleep); actually spawns scripts/session-tick.cjs as a real child process and observes its behavior via the marker file and/or a mocked PostgREST endpoint. MUST be validated by running from the MAIN repo tree, not the EXEC worktree (vitest config excludes **/.worktrees/**, so a test authored only in the worktree would silently never run in CI).
acceptance_criteria: New spawn-and-observe test file(s) never touch a live/real claude_sessions row -- verified by the assert-before-mutate guard rejecting any non-test-prefixed session_id.
Full test run completes in well under 60s total (no reliance on the real 30s TICK_MS).
Tests pass when run from the main repo tree (not just the EXEC worktree).

functional_requirements.title: Stage flag re-enablement: LEO_DORMANCY_WATCHDOG_ENABLED only
functional_requirements.description: Once FR-1/FR-2/FR-3 are proven by FR-4's tests, flip LEO_DORMANCY_WATCHDOG_ENABLED=on (the interim-mitigation SD's gate, sole consumer scripts/stale-session-sweep.cjs:697, advisory-only -- files a feedback row, no automated action). Do NOT flip LEO_MASKED_STALL_DETECT=on in this SD -- its consumers (coordinator-capacity-forecast.mjs, lib/coordinator/detectors.cjs) drive actual capacity DECISIONS, a materially higher blast radius that deserves its own tracked follow-up SD with a proper monitoring window, not a bundled flip here.
acceptance_criteria: LEO_DORMANCY_WATCHDOG_ENABLED=on is set (in .env or the relevant runtime config) as part of this SD's completion.
A canary check confirms at least one new session's tick behavior post-merge shows no false-positive fleet_dormancy alert.
A completion flag or follow-up-SD note explicitly tracks LEO_MASKED_STALL_DETECT re-enablement as separate, deferred work -- not silently dropped.

technical_requirements.title: No mutation of live production session rows during testing
technical_requirements.description: All new tests operate against a disposable table or a strictly-guarded test-prefixed session_id. 11+ live sessions (including the ones authoring this SD) depend on session-tick.cjs today; a test that touches real rows risks destabilizing the fleet mid-development.

technical_requirements.title: Preserve the 18b90582 incident fix
technical_requirements.description: The stop-on-terminal-status behavior (release detection via 0-row PATCH) must remain intact and test-covered after FR-1's filter widening -- this is a hard regression-prevention requirement, not just a nice-to-have.

technical_requirements.title: Cross-platform correctness
technical_requirements.description: FR-2's DB-based re-query fix has no OS-specific dependency, unlike the WMI approach it replaces -- verify the fix does not silently break on non-Windows platforms where the old Windows-only guard previously no-op'd.

test_scenarios.scenario: Live-spawned tick still stops on active->released transition
test_scenarios.expected: cleanupAndExit fires, tick stops (18b90582 fix preserved)
Tick adopts the new live PID from claude_sessions.pid and continues, instead of exiting after MAX_PARENT_MISSES

test_scenarios.scenario: First-tick fire against a pre-released row
test_scenarios.expected: Row status remains released, not resurrected to active

test_scenarios.scenario: Full existing session-tick test suite + new spawn-and-observe suite together
test_scenarios.expected: All pass, run from the main tree, under 60s total

acceptance_criteria: session-tick.cjs no longer self-exits when a session's status legitimately drifts to idle.
The 18b90582 stop-on-released fix is preserved and test-covered.
rediscoverParentPid()'s Windows PID-rotation recovery actually works (proven by a live test), replacing the previously structurally-inert WMI approach.
The first-tick POST can no longer resurrect a released row.
New spawn-and-observe tests exist, never touch live data, and run in well under 60s from the main tree.
LEO_DORMANCY_WATCHDOG_ENABLED is flipped on with a canary check; LEO_MASKED_STALL_DETECT re-enablement is explicitly deferred and tracked, not silently dropped.

risks.risk: Widening the status filter could still be too broad if a status value other than idle/released/completed exists in production that should also be treated as terminal.
risks.mitigation: Query the live claude_sessions status distribution before finalizing the IN-list; VALIDATION sub-agent already confirmed active:8/idle:3/released:989 as the current live distribution -- re-verify at EXEC time and check for any other status values in use.

risks.risk: New spawn-and-observe tests could flake in CI if timing assumptions (env-overridden TICK_MS) do not hold under CI resource contention.
risks.mitigation: Use deterministic completion signals (marker file existence/content, not fixed sleeps) and a generous but bounded (<=10s) time-box per test.

risks.risk: This SD touches a daemon that live, in-flight worker sessions (including the one authoring this SD) depend on for liveness tracking.
risks.mitigation: All test infrastructure is strictly isolated from live rows (TR-1); the actual code fix is small and surgical; canary-verify against one new session before declaring complete.

consumers: scripts/coordinator-capacity-forecast.mjs (masked-stall detector, gated, unaffected by this SD)
data_contracts: claude_sessions.status IN-list widened for the steady-state PATCH filter only -- no schema change
claude_sessions.pid remains the read source for FR-2's re-query, unchanged write contract
runtime_config: LEO_MASKED_STALL_DETECT (explicitly NOT flipped by this SD)
observability_rollout: post-merge canary check on a new session

system_architecture.summary: Three surgical fixes to scripts/session-tick.cjs's steady-state loop, PID-rediscovery, and first-tick self-heal paths, backed by new real-process spawn-and-observe test infrastructure that proves runtime behavior instead of static source-text matching.

components.name: scripts/session-tick.cjs
components.change: MODIFIED -- widened steady-state filter (FR-1), DB-based rediscoverParentPid (FR-2), guarded first-tick POST (FR-3)

components.name: tests/unit/session-tick-release-on-exit.test.mjs
components.change: MODIFIED -- FR-2's filter regex updated to match the widened IN-list

components.name: new spawn-and-observe test file(s)
components.change: NEW -- real child-process spawn tests for FR-1/FR-2/FR-3, disposable-data only

implementation_approach.summary: LEAD-phase sub-agents (Explore + VALIDATION) already confirmed exact line numbers, corrected a naive-fix hazard against an existing incident-closing test, discovered a reusable DB-based mechanism for the PID-rediscovery fix, and set explicit safety rails for test infrastructure given 11+ live dogfooding sessions.
steps: Re-verify the live claude_sessions status distribution at EXEC time (confirm active/idle/released/completed are the only values in use before finalizing the IN-list).
Implement FR-1 (widen filter), update the existing FR-2 test regex to match.
Implement FR-2 (DB-based rediscoverParentPid), remove the WMI/PowerShell approach, confirm no other code path depends on it.
Implement FR-3 (guard first-tick POST against resurrection).
Build the spawn-and-observe test harness (FR-4) with disposable-data guards, env-overridable timing, hard time-box.
Run the full existing + new test suites from the MAIN tree (not the worktree) to confirm vitest actually picks them up.
Flip LEO_DORMANCY_WATCHDOG_ENABLED=on, canary-check one new session, explicitly defer LEO_MASKED_STALL_DETECT re-enablement via a completion flag or tracked follow-up note.

smoke_test_steps.instruction: Run the new spawn-and-observe test suite plus tests/unit/session-tick-release-on-exit.test.mjs and tests/unit/session-tick-heartbeat.test.mjs together
smoke_test_steps.expected_outcome: All pass, total run time well under 60s, run from the main repo tree

smoke_test_steps.instruction: Grep scripts/session-tick.cjs for the widened filter string
smoke_test_steps.expected_outcome: status=in.(active,idle) found at the steady-state PATCH URL construction

smoke_test_steps.instruction: Grep scripts/session-tick.cjs for any remaining Win32_Process/CommandLine WMI logic
smoke_test_steps.expected_outcome: No match -- the WMI-based rediscoverParentPid() has been replaced by the DB re-query
